_comment: Urban Search & Rescue protocol for incident_type=structural_collapse. Operational guidance for trained responders, paraphrased from the INSARAG methodology (no verbatim handbook text). Sorting/extrication procedure only - no patient diagnosis or treatment. Loaded by matcher.py at startup; keywords drive the forgiving fallback match on needs/notes.
title: Urban Search & Rescue - structural collapse

guidance.action: Complete a scene size-up before anyone approaches: building type, damage pattern, visible voids, and active hazards (gas, electricity, unstable walls, aftershock risk).
guidance.rationale: Every entry decision flows from the size-up; skipping it is the main cause of secondary responder casualties.

guidance.action: Isolate utilities before any search or powered-tool work: shut off gas at the main and de-energize electrical feeds to the structure.
guidance.rationale: Ignition of leaking gas and contact with live wiring are the two fastest ways a rescue site kills its rescuers.

guidance.action: Interview bystanders, family, and neighbours for how many occupants were inside and their last known locations within the structure.
guidance.rationale: Occupant intelligence focuses the void search where live victims are most likely to be.

guidance.action: Run a surface search, then hail: call out, order total silence on site, and listen for knocking or voices; repeat from several points around the structure.
guidance.rationale: Locating survivors by sound narrows the dig site before rescuers are committed to slow, risky breaching.

guidance.action: Shore or crib every void before entry, and never place any part of the body under a load that is not supported.
guidance.rationale: Unshored voids fail when disturbed; shoring is what keeps a rescue from becoming a second collapse.

guidance.action: Prioritize extrication by signs of life and accessibility: reachable victims with confirmed signs of life first, and assign heavy-lifting resources to them.
guidance.rationale: Ordering work by survivability and access maximizes lives saved per hour of scarce rescue capacity.

guidance.action: Apply the standard INSARAG worksite marking to every searched structure, recording team, time, hazards, and victims found.
guidance.rationale: Consistent marking stops different teams and shifts from re-searching the same site.

safety_flags: Isolate gas and electricity before using powered tools
Post an aftershock lookout and agree an evacuation signal before entry
Never work under an unsupported load
